items.title: Melaka snake incident: Man forces reptile into car at Stadthuys tourist spot
items.summary: MELAKA: A young woman says she was left traumatised after a man allegedly forced a snake into her car near the Stadthuys in Bandar Hilir on Monday night, in an incident that has since gone viral on social media. Nik Hanis Allysa Abdullah, 21, said the incident occurred around 11pm when she, her younger sibling and a friend had stopped near the historic landmark before being approached by a man carrying a snake. Fortunately, nothing worse happened," she said.

items.title: Bank Negara imposes RM520,000 fine on Aeon Credit for sanctions breaches
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Negara Malaysia has fined Aeon Credit Service (M) Bhd RM520,000 for failure to comply with targeted financial sanctions. "Aeon Credit failed to reject the potential match despite it being a positive match. Aeon Credit has paid the required amount on April 16.

items.title: H5 bird flu detected in second Australia state
items.summary: SYDNEY: Scientists have detected the highly contagious H5 bird flu strain in a second Australian state, the nation's agriculture minister said on Wednesday. Australia was for years the only continental landmass to be free of the H5 strain, which has caused severe disease and high death rates in poultry and wild birds worldwide. Two H5 cases have been confirmed in migratory birds found in the state of Western Australia, with officials now warning of a third infection found hundreds of kilometres (miles) away in South Australia.

items.title: Online scam losses surge to RM2.97b in 2025, says Home Ministry as fraud trend worsens across Malaysia
items.summary: The Home Ministry (KDN) said according to the comparison of various modus operandi, non-existent investments consistently recorded the largest losses with a record of RM848.62 million in 2024, jumping to RM1.46 billion in 2025 and RM361.63 million this year. According to KDN, the trend was closely followed by telecommunications crime which recorded RM497.12 million (2024), RM802.47 million (2025) and RM235.63 million (as of May 2026), while love scams continued to record losses of RM45.87 million (2024), RM47.44 million (2025) and RM17.76 million for the early period of this year. From a geographical aspect, he said Selangor and Kuala Lumpur continued to record the highest losses, with Selangor jumping from RM446.16 million (2024) to RM986.79 million (2025), while Kuala Lumpur increased from RM293.30 million (2024) to RM782.86 million (2025).

items.title: Stats Dept: Domestic tourism spending climbs 15.8pc to RM34b in Q1 2026 amid festive travel boost
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 24 — Domestic tourism expenditure recorded a double-digit growth of 15.8 per cent year-on-year to RM34 billion in the first quarter of 2026 (1Q 2026), according to the Bulletin of Malaysia Domestic Tourism Survey - First Quarter 2026 released by the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) today. In a statement, DOSM said Malaysia’s domestic tourism maintained its growth momentum in the first three months of 2026, with total domestic visitors reaching 74.7 million, reflecting a year-on-year increase of 7.2 per cent. The performance was further supported by a 0.9 per cent quarter-on-quarter growth compared to the fourth quarter of 2025, indicating continued stability in domestic travel demand.

items.title: Two policemen badly hurt in attack by violent drug addict
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Two policemen from the Selama police headquarters' Narcotics Crime Investigation Department (NCID) were seriously injured following a knife attack by a suspected drug addict in Kampung Sungai Jernih, Ijok, yesterday. Sources said the constable suffered serious facial injuries while the sergeant sustained wounds to his head, arm and abdomen. One source said the suspect, in his 30s, had tried to flee when he noticed the presence of policemen near his hut.

items.title: World Cup gambling crackdown nets 1,156 arrests
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have arrested 1,156 people and carried out 868 raids in a city-wide crackdown on illegal gambling activities linked to the FIFA World Cup 2026. He said the operation, conducted between Jan 1 and June 23, targeted football betting syndicates, online gambling operators, illegal lottery activities, gambling dens, outdoor gambling activities and illegal calling centres linked to gambling operations. Fadil said police also carried out 322 raids on online gambling operations, leading to 341 arrests, while 486 raids on illegal lottery activities resulted in 487 arrests.

items.title: Settled is settled, Sanusi tells Bersatu over Wawasan admission into PN
items.summary: PN election director Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor told Bersatu that the matter had already been settled at the coalition's meeting and should not be revisited afterwards. "It is already settled. I said the day before yesterday that it was already settled and the meeting concluded well.

items.title: Former IT engineer fined RM18,000 for abusing Budi95 subsidy
items.summary: BESUT: A former information technology engineer was fined RM18,000, in default 18 months' jail, by the magistrate's court today after pleading guilty to three counts of using another person's identity card. For the first charge, he was accused of using another person's MyKad at a petrol station in Alor Lintang at 11.33am on June 1. Nurliyana imposed a fine of RM6,000, in default six months' jail, for each charge.

items.title: Tempers flare in Dewan Rakyat over Kelantan convoy, illegal racing link
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tempers flared in the Dewan Rakyat today after a government member of parliament linked a motorcycle convoy in Kelantan to the rise of illegal street racing among youths. Khoo Poay Tiong (PH–Kota Melaka) made the remarks while debating the Road Transport (Amendment) Bill 2026, which addresses offences related to illegal racing and speed testing. He described a motorcycle convoy organised by Melor assemblyman Wan Rohimi Wan Daud as unacceptable, claiming that such activities could encourage illegal racing behaviour among young people.

items.title: Motorcyclist found dead in drain near Karangkraf office
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: A factory worker was found dead after the motorcycle he was riding skidded and plunged into a drain along Persiaran Selangor near the Karangkraf office in Section 15 here. Shah Alam police chief Assistant Commissioner Sarudin Samah said the victim, a man in his 40s, was found together with his motorcycle around 11.40pm yesterday. Sarudin said a post-mortem conducted at Shah Alam Hospital at 9am today determined the cause of death as chest injuries sustained in a road crash.

items.title: AI could cut retail operating costs by up to 30pct: Mydin director
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Artificial intelligence (AI) is a powerful tool for retailers seeking to manage rising operating costs, with potential savings of up to 30 per cent in certain processes, Mydin Mohamed Holdings Bhd director Malik Murad Ali said. Retailers are facing mounting cost pressures, particularly from labour and supply chain expenses, as transportation and operating costs continue to rise. To mitigate these challenges, Malik said AI is helping businesses optimise operations, improve productivity and preserve profit margins without having to pass on additional costs to consumers.

items.title: MyMall, TikTok Shop drive digital growth among MSMEs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government is continuing to strengthen the country's digital entrepreneurship ecosystem through various initiatives aimed at enhancing the competitiveness, productivity and market access of local entrepreneurs, both domestically and internationally. Deputy Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Minister Datuk Mohamad Alamin said the efforts are being implemented under the Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) Strategic Plan 2030, which focuses on digital transformation and sustainable business development. He said the approach is intended to expand opportunities for local entrepreneurs in the growing digital economy, including through online marketing platforms and capacity-building programmes.

items.title: No proof of electrocution in UUM student's death, court rules
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Universiti Utara Malaysia (UUM) was not liable for the death of student Vinosiny R. Sivakumar, who was found dead in a dormitory room in 2022, the High Court has ruled. Judge Dr Johan Lee made the ruling when dismissing a RM3.05 million lawsuit against the university filed by Vinosiny's father, R. Sivakumar Rajoo, recently. The court said the plaintiff's case was not supported by objective technical evidence, adding that independent investigations established the safety of the university's electrical installations.

items.title: MRCB secures consent judgment in Shah Alam Stadium suit; case against blogger to continue
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — Malaysian Resources Corporation Bhd (MRCB) has secured a consent judgment against one of the defendants in its lawsuit alleging a conspiracy to cause economic damage through online posts about the demolition and redevelopment of the Shah Alam Stadium. According to a statement, the consent judgment was entered at the Shah Alam High Court on June 22 against self-described activist Abdul Razak Ismail, one of two defendants named in the suit. MRCB had sued Abdul Razak and blogger Murray Hunter over a series of posts published on Facebook and Hunter’s Substack platform that questioned the stadium demolition and redevelopment project.

items.title: No new subsidy burden under Budi Madani Diesel: Liew Chin Tong
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The streamlined Budi Madani Diesel mechanism will not expand subsidy programmes or increase government subsidy expenditure, ensuring fiscal discipline is maintained. He said all diesel subsidies are now placed under the Budi Madani framework, including assistance that was previously channelled through other methods to eligible recipients. Previously, diesel users received cash subsidies and additional assistance separately.

items.title: Che'gu Bard ordered to enter defence over defamation against King
items.summary: Judge Noor Ruwena Md Nurdin made the ruling after allowing the prosecution's appeal against the Sessions Court's decision last November, which had acquitted the 47-year-old at the end of the prosecution's case. "Accordingly, the court orders that the case be remitted to the Sessions Court before the same judge for the defence stage to proceed," she said. On Nov 4 last year, Sessions Court judge Norma Ismail had acquitted him after ruling that the prosecution failed to establish a prima facie case.

items.title: Johor election: Excitement grows as BN leaders, supporters gather for candidate announcements
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Johor Umno headquarters is all abuzz as Barisan Nasional leaders, members and supporters have begun gathering for the much-awaited announcement of candidates for the 16th state election. The announcement was later postponed to 4.30pm. Among those seen were Umno treasurer Datuk Seri Tengku Adnan Tengku Mansor and Datuk Seri Dr Adham Baba.

items.title: Haim Hilman to appear in court over university admission claims
items.summary: ALOR SETAR: Jitra assemblyman Dr Haim Hilman Abdullah will be charged at the Seremban magistrate's court tomorrow over allegations related to claims he previously made concerning access to higher education. Haim Hilman, who is also Kedah's Industry and Investment, Higher Education, Science, Technology and Innovation committee chairman, said he would appear in court at 9am to face the charge. The media had also reported that the Higher Education Ministry viewed seriously several allegations concerning the public university admission system that had been repeatedly raised by Haim Hilman and was considering legal action over the matter.

items.title: Major resurfacing works for pothole-hit Jalan Lahat to start next month
items.summary: IPOH, June 24 — Road users can expect smoother, safer journeys soon as a major resurfacing and pothole repair project is set to begin along the heavily trafficked Jalan Lahat here. Menglembu assemblyman Steven Chaw said the RM2.6 million project, funded through the Malaysian Road Records Information System (MARRIS) allocation, will cover approximately 1.9km in each direction, bringing the total resurfacing length to nearly 4km. Thousands of motorists travelling to and from Ipoh use this road every day.

items.title: France heatwave triggers major blackout, 68,000 homes hit
items.summary: RENNES, France: Europe's record-breaking heatwave left around 68,000 households without electricity in northwestern France on Wednesday, authorities said, in the country's first major power outage of the latest bout of extreme weather. The outage, which involved a transformer on the electricity grid, was related to extreme temperatures and did not injure anyone, the prefecture in the coastal department of Finistere said in a statement. Up to 106,000 clients of the French power network were left without power by late Tuesday, as the continent's sweltering heatwave pushed the country into its hottest day ever.

items.title: Govt to assess Budi Diesel quota requests using actual consumption data, says minister
items.summary: Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said any decision regarding the programme, including the issue of increasing quotas, would be made based on actual usage data. “When we first started implementing the RON95 subsidy, some said the quota was insufficient. Amir Hamzah said previous experience with targeted subsidy programmes, including those for the e-hailing sector, showed that the government is always prepared to make adjustments based on actual usage data.

items.title: Thailand's 'Michael Jackson' jailed over deadly drink-driving crash
items.summary: BANGKOK: A Thai court jailed on Wednesday a singer dubbed Thailand's Michael Jackson for two years for killing two people while driving while drunk. The 64-year-old singer was driving his vehicle across a bridge in Bangkok in October 2024 when he hit a stationary motorbike. A criminal court in Bangkok on Wednesday sentenced Manasawin to two years in prison for "drunk driving resulting in loss of life."

items.title: Two injured after motorcycle crashes into car in Kuala Selangor
items.summary: KUALA SELANGOR: Police are investigating a road accident that left a motorcyclist and his pillion rider injured after their motorcycle crashed into the rear of a car along Jalan Kuala Selangor-Kuala Lumpur on Sunday. Kuala Selangor police chief Superintendent Azaharudin Tajudin said the district traffic complaints counter received a report on the incident at 8.36pm involving a Proton Iriz and a Yamaha Y15ZR motorcycle. He said investigations revealed that the crash occurred following a misunderstanding between the Yamaha rider and another motorcyclist riding a similar motorcycle while travelling towards Kuala Selangor.

items.title: 14 Malaysians receive Yang di-Pertuan Agong Scholarship 2026
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today presented the prestigious Yang di-Pertuan Agong Scholarship (BYDPA) 2026 to 14 outstanding Malaysians at Istana Negara. The recipients comprise seven doctoral (PhD) candidates and seven master's by research students, reflecting the government's continued emphasis on developing high-impact talent for the nation's strategic sectors. Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Wan Ahmad Dahlan Abdul Aziz said the government, through the PSD, would continue strengthening talent development initiatives aligned with national strategic priorities and public sector needs.

items.title: Education Ministry preparing for larger 2027 Year One intake with new classrooms and teacher recruitment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — The Ministry of Education (MOE) is stepping up preparations for the intake of two Year One cohorts in 2027, including the construction of 2,596 new classrooms and the recruitment of 3,150 contract of service (COS) teachers, based on current registration figures. Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh said the ministry has received 73,386 Year One registration applications for six-year-old children for the 2027 school session, while the number of seven-year-old children stands at 405,033. He said the combined total of 478,419 applications represents a 12.07 per cent increase compared with Year One enrolment in 2026, which involved only the seven-year-old cohort.

items.title: Fahmi: Social media age checks aimed at keeping under-16s off platforms
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — The Child Protection Code (CPC) establishes a safe, practical and responsible age-verification mechanism to ensure only eligible users are allowed access to social media platforms. He said both codes aim to create a safer online environment, particularly by protecting children from online risks and harm. “Under the CPC, licensed service providers offering social media services are required to implement age-verification mechanisms rather than identity verification to ensure compliance with the minimum age requirement.

items.title: Yinson's FSO vessel ready for Vietnam deployment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Yinson Production has reached a milestone with the naming of its floating storage and offloading (FSO) vessel FSO PTSC Lac Da Vang ahead of its deployment to the Lac Da Vang project offshore Vietnam. The vessel was contracted by Murphy Cuu Long Bac Oil Co Ltd, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Murphy Oil Corp, to provide storage and offloading services for the Lac Da Vang field in Block 15-1/05 offshore Vietnam. Yinson Production chief executive officer Flemming Guiducci Grønnegaard said the naming marks an important milestone in the journey towards deployment and first operations at the Lac Da Vang field.

items.title: Business closures, downsizing led to 42,807 job losses this year, says Ramanan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — A total of 42,807 workers nationwide were reported to have lost their jobs from January to June 12 this year, said Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan. He said based on statistics from the Social Security Organisation (Socso), business closures and company downsizing were identified as the main causes of retrenchment, affecting 17,485 people or 40.85 per cent. “Kuala Lumpur recorded the highest number of job losses at 30 per cent or 12,844 people, followed by Selangor with 12,360 people, and Johor, which recorded a job loss rate of 8.1 per cent or 3,468 people,” he said during the Question Time at the Dewan Rakyat today.

items.title: Private university student charged with trafficking RM1.17mil worth of cannabis buds [WATCH]
items.summary: JITRA: A private university student was charged in the magistrate's court here today with trafficking 14.63kg of cannabis buds worth RM1.17 million at the Bukit Kayu Hitam Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) Complex last week. No plea was recorded from Ishvherr Singh Jagjit Singh, 24, after the charge was read to him before magistrate Nor Syaliati Mohd Sobri, as the case falls under the jurisdiction of the High Court. Inspector Sharifah Nurlin Atiqah Wan Idrus prosecuted, while the accused was represented by Datuk Naran Singh.

items.title: ByteDance seeks US$20 billion in its largest-ever offshore loan
items.summary: BEIJING: Chinese technology company ByteDance, the developer of TikTok, is in preliminary talks with banks for its largest offshore loan of about US$20 billion, Bloomberg News reported on Wednesday, citing people familiar with the matter. The company has approached banks for a loan that may carry a three-year tenor, with an option to extend it to as long as five years, the report said. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Former food delivery CEO rebuilds life after company collapse
items.summary: KOTA BARU: From humble beginnings, Indera Aria Wee Jeeny rose to become chief executive officer (CEO) of a food delivery platform company before losing it all. Although he did not come from a well-off family, Indera, 40, once found success as CEO of a food ordering and delivery platform company that began operations in October 2019. Sharing his experience, the father of two said his life changed completely after the collapse, leaving him unable to afford food.

items.title: PH man challenges Onn Hafiz to name Felda 'blockers'
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor PH communications director Sheikh Umar Bagharib Ali today challenged Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi to identify those who allegedly prevented him from entering a Felda settlement. Sheikh Umar accused the Johor BN chairman of creating a political narrative ahead of the July 11 state election. However, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, who oversees Felda and Felda Global Ventures (FGV), denied that any instruction had been issued to prevent Onn Hafiz from entering any Felda settlement.

items.title: Can Pakistan's peacekeeping role in Iran war give it an economic dividend?
items.summary: PAKISTAN'S role in brokering a peace deal in the Iran war has led to widespread diplomatic acclaim that could bring Islamabad some economic benefits, but analysts question whether such gains can help resolve the fault lines in its economy. Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and army chief Field Marshal Asim Munir attended talks between Iran and the US in the Swiss town of Buergenstock last weekend, the culmination of Pakistan's months-long role in one of the world's most consequential diplomatic negotiations. "A nation that delivers stability at home and helps advance stability abroad becomes a more credible destination for investment," said Khurram Schehzad, adviser to Pakistan's finance minister.

items.title: 'It used to be noises, now it shows itself'
items.summary: Nur Aina Amalina Karim, 31, said she, her husband Mohd Faisal Usop, 31, their four children aged between seven and 13, and a 15-year-old nephew began experiencing what they described as "mystical disturbances" on May 25. The family, who live in Kampung Kutan Sungai, said they started hearing unexplained sounds several days after Hari Raya Aidiladha. Nur Aina, a shop assistant, said the incidents had left her children frightened.

items.title: 3,150 teachers hired on contract to meet 2027 schooling needs
items.summary: However, he said current registrations for the cohort stand at 73,386 pupils, resulting in a revised immediate staffing requirement. "The ministry is working to ensure that reserve candidates are offered appointments as soon as possible." Regarding reserve candidates, Wong said there are two batches of reserve applicants — from July 2025 and December 2025 — totalling 4,417 candidates.

items.title: [UPDATED] Sanusi: PN settles all seat negotiations for Johor election
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: PN election director Datuk Seri Sanusi Md Nor today announced that seat negotiations for the Johor election among the coalition's component parties have been concluded, with 34 overlapping seats resolved. Sanusi, who is also Kedah menteri besar, said Bersatu would contest the largest number of seats, although he declined to disclose the coalition's overall seat allocation. "Alhamdulillah, negotiations among PN component parties have been completed.

items.title: 110 investigation papers opened under Printing Presses Act as of June 22
items.summary: Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said all cases are still under investigation. She also asked whether the government would review the law to safeguard freedom of expression. Saifuddin said the Federal Constitution does not grant absolute freedom to citizens but instead provides that such rights are subject to restrictions deemed necessary by Parliament to safeguard security, public order or morality.

items.title: National Unity Ministry unveils plans to transform Malaysia’s museum sector
items.summary: “In addition, the Madani Government has approved the development of the Malaysia Natural History Museum in Putrajaya, thereby realising an aspiration planned since 1989,” he said at the opening ceremony of the National-Level International Museum Day Festival 2026 at Padang Akasia, Angsana Johor Bahru Mall, today. He said the development of the natural history museum is among the strategic initiatives aimed at strengthening preservation and education related to biodiversity and Malaysia’s natural heritage. “To date, the Natural History Museum houses a total of 76,666 specimens, comprising 73,934 zoological collections, 2,075 botanical collections, and 657 geological collections.

items.title: Perkeso recorded over 96pc compliance in claims processing last year, says Ramanan
items.summary: He said a two-day processing period was set for Funeral Benefit and Temporary Disablement Benefit claims, while Permanent Disablement Benefit, Invalidity Pension, Survivor’s Pension and Dependant’s Benefit claims are processed within three days. Mohd Isam wanted to know the official processing period for Perkeso claims, compliance levels and the use of digitalisation to expedite claims for contributors. Ramanan said Perkeso continuously enhances its operational systems through digitalisation, including the LINDUNG Faedah Perkeso portal, to improve claims processing and benefit payments.

items.title: Anthony Loke urges Malaysia to take lead in drones, air taxis and emerging aviation sector
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — Malaysia must position itself as an active participant in the rapidly emerging low altitude economy (LAE) sector, which could potentially be the new growth engine, Transport Minister Anthony Loke said today. He said Malaysia has a solid base in traditional aviation and aerospace industries, but has not leveraged opportunities for maintenance, repair and overhaul (MRO) and manufacturing in the LAE sector. But for LAE, the standards and frameworks are being developed globally, and Malaysia must take this opportunity to write our own frameworks and standards,” he said at the inaugural Low Altitude Economy Forum 2026 here today.

items.title: Act 792 Amendment Bill seeks to extend coverage to child sexual offences committed abroad
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — The Bill to amend the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017 (Act 792) seeks to extend the application of the act to offences committed overseas by individuals with ties to Malaysia. According to the Bill’s text published on the Parliament website today, the amendment to section 3 of Act 792 provides that offences committed outside Malaysia are punishable under the act if they involve a child who is a Malaysian citizen or a permanent resident. The amendment further provides that the Act will also apply to sexual offences against children committed overseas by Malaysian citizens or permanent residents.

items.title: Personal dispute, financial issues believed to be motive in Bandar Sunway private college student murder, says Selangor top cop
items.summary: Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said initial investigations found that the suspect and victim are believed to have known each other, were in a relationship, and had been living together, according to Astro Awani. “At this early stage of investigation, police believe the motive is related to personal issues involving emotions and financial spending,” he said at a press conference at the Selangor police headquarters today. Media reports yesterday said the victim’s body was found with multiple injuries at a condominium unit.

items.title: Kedah exco to face court over higher education access issue linked to ‘backdoor’ admissions claims
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — Kedah Higher Education Exco Haim Hilman Abdullah is set to face charges at the Seremban Magistrates’ Court on Thursday over issues related to higher education access raised previously. “I will attend the Seremban Magistrates’ Court on June 25, 2026 at 9am to face charges related to higher education access issues that I raised in defence of the rights of Malaysian children, especially from B40 and M40 families,” he said. The Higher Education Ministry had earlier said it viewed the allegations seriously and was considering legal action to defend the integrity of the country’s higher education system.

items.title: Malaysia records 42,807 job losses as 697,000 roles at risk from AI without urgent upskilling, says HR minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan has warned that up to 697,000 jobs in Malaysia could be significantly affected by artificial intelligence (AI), digitalisation and the green economy within the next three to five years if workers fail to upgrade their skills. “The findings show that 697,000 jobs are expected to be highly affected by AI, digitalisation and the green economy if workers do not upskill within the next three to five years. Ramanan then said the government was prioritising reskilling and upskilling programmes, particularly for workers at risk of displacement due to technological change.

items.title: King receives Chief Justice, Army chief in separate audiences at Istana Negara today
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today held separate audiences with the Chief Justice and the Commander of the Malaysian Army at Istana Negara here. In the first audience, His Majesty granted an audience to Chief Justice Tun Wan Ahmad Farid Wan Salleh, in a meeting held at the royal palace earlier today. Wan Ahmad Farid was appointed the 17th Chief Justice on July 28 last year.

items.title: Malaysia’s Low Altitude Economy framework to be unveiled by Q4 2026, says Anthony Loke
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 24 — The regulatory, operational and governance framework for Malaysia’s Low Altitude Economy (LAE) ecosystem is expected to be unveiled by the fourth quarter of this year, the Transport Ministry said today. “We hope to announce the blueprint (framework) by the end of this year,” Transport Minister Anthony Loke told reporters after launching the inaugural Low Altitude Economy Forum 2026 here. With Malaysia’s approach towards LAE guided by a phased and coordinated roadmap, Loke said it reflects the federal government’s commitment to developing the LAE in a structured, safe and coordinated manner, while also creating an enabling environment for industry participation and investment.

items.title: Bus carrying 32 college students skids in Kulim, driver injured
items.summary: KULIM, June 24 — A bus driver was injured while 32 passengers escaped unhurt after the vehicle skidded along Jalan Lebuh Kulim Techno City (KTC) near here today. Kulim district police chief Supt Syamsul Sinring said the bus, carrying 32 college students, was travelling from Taman KTC 1 towards Kulim Hi-Tech Park (KHTP) when the incident occurred at 7.30 am. Syamsul said that investigations into the incident were ongoing.

items.title: No formal extradition request from Bangladesh for Bestinet founder Aminul Islam, says home minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — Putrajaya has not received any formal request to extradite Bestinet Sdn Bhd founder Datuk Seri Mohamed Aminul Islam, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail told Parliament through a written reply published last night. Malaysia and Bangladesh do not have an extradition treaty but has a legal provision to do so under the Extradition Act 1992, Saifuddin said in the reply. “If Malaysia has no (extradition) treaty with another country, a request can still be done under Section 3 of the Act.

items.title: Anwar pays tribute to former lecturer Abu Bakar, prominent Malay literary figure
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has expressed his profound sadness over the passing of academic figure Professor Emeritus Datuk Abu Bakar Abd Hamid, who died last Sunday. In a Facebook post today, he described the late Abu Bakar, a former deputy vice-chancellor of Universiti Malaya (UM), as a prominent figure in Malay literature and instrumental in the establishment of Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia (UKM). As one of Abu Bakar’s former students, Anwar also expressed his sorrow over the loss of a teacher who was deeply loved and cherished.

items.title: PAC urges diplomatic push after Norway revokes LCS missile licence despite 95pc payment by Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — The Public Accounts Committee (PAC) has raised serious concerns over Malaysia’s Littoral Combat Ship (LCS) project after Norway revoked an export licence for missiles intended for the vessels, despite the government having already paid a vast amount of the contract value. PAC chair Datuk Wira Mas Ermieyati Samsudin said the committee is urging the government to intensify diplomatic engagement with Norway and explore all available legal avenues to protect national interests. Mas Ermieyati then said the committee also urged the government to pursue negotiations or legal action, where necessary, to obtain appropriate compensation in accordance with the terms of the contract.

items.title: Puad Zarkashi hints at key announcement on BN candidate list ahead of Johor nomination day
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 24 — Johor state legislative assembly speaker Datuk Mohd Puad Zarkashi hinted that he will make an important announcement on June 27, following the latest developments in the selection of Barisan Nasional (BN) candidates for the state election. “Today, Johor BN will announce the list of candidates that will contest in the 2026 Johor state election. The latest developments in Johor Umno are worrying,” he said.

items.title: King contributes RM500,000 to help over 1,000 Sabah fire victims
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today graciously bestowed a personal contribution of RM500,000 to assist victims affected by fires in Kudat and Sandakan, Sabah. His Majesty witnessed the presentation of the contribution by Sultan Ibrahim Johor Foundation (YSIJ) Trustee Tan Sri Avinderjit Singh to Sabah Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor during a ceremony at Istana Negara here. According to incident records, a fire at Kampung Gumpa in Kudat on April 16 destroyed eight houses and affected 67 victims from 18 households.

items.title: Competition Act amendments to be tabled next week to boost MyCC enforcement powers, says deputy minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — Amendments to the Competition Act 2010 (Act 712) will be tabled for a second reading in Parliament next week as Putrajaya moves to strengthen the law and expand the enforcement powers of the Malaysia Competition Commission (MyCC). Deputy Minister of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Datuk Fuziah Salleh said the proposed changes aim to enhance MyCC’s ability to tackle anti-competitive practices more effectively. “God-willing, amendments to the Competition Act 2010 (Act 712) will be tabled for second reading next week.

items.title: Puncak Borneo water system has spare capacity to supply more villages, says Sarawak minister
items.summary: PADAWAN, June 24 — The newly completed Puncak Borneo Acute Water Supply System has sufficient spare capacity to extend treated water supply to more villages that have yet to receive piped water, said Utility and Telecommunication Minister Datuk Seri Julaihi Narawi. “Among the areas already benefiting from the project are Kampung Gerung, Kampung Karu, Kampung Gayu, Kampung Bayur, Kampung Jambu, Kampung Sigandar, Kampung Braang Payang, Kampung Serumah, Teng Bukap, Kampung Bidak, Kampung Krian, Kampung Sira and SMK Padawan,” he said. Julaihi said the Puncak Borneo Acute Water Supply System was built as an immediate solution by the Sarawak government following delays in implementing a federal water supply project approved in 2016.

items.title: Lorry driver loses over RM80,000 after fake job offer on Facebook
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, June 24 — A lorry driver lost RM80,580 after falling victim to an online part-time job scam syndicate. “However, after completing several subsequent tasks, the suspect asked the victim to make payments purportedly as management fees to withdraw profits amounting to RM400,000, which the victim was told he would receive,” he said in a statement today. Azli said the victim made 21 transactions totalling RM80,580 to six different bank accounts between June 11 and 22, using money borrowed from friends.

items.title: Police arrest seven after roadside brawl in Jasin leaves two injured
items.summary: JASIN, June 24 — Police have detained seven individuals, comprising six men and one woman, to assist in investigations into a brawl that left two people injured by the roadside near a bus stop along Jalan Gapam in Bemban here yesterday. Jasin district police chief Supt Lee Robert said the suspects, aged between 20 and 29, were arrested in several separate raids conducted around Melaka between 3 pm and 6.50 pm yesterday. Yesterday, Lee was reported as saying that two men suffered serious injuries after they were allegedly assaulted in a fight involving two groups of men by the roadside near a bus stop along Jalan Gapam.

items.title: Malaysia poised to tap Low Altitude Economy boom, says Anthony Loke
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 24 — Malaysia is well-positioned to play an active role in developing a vibrant Low Altitude Economy (LAE) ecosystem, as Putrajaya moves to unlock new economic opportunities from drones, advanced air mobility and other emerging aviation technologies. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said Malaysia possessed a strong foundation for developing a LAE ecosystem, citing the country’s mature aviation industry, strong engineering capabilities, growing digital economy and strategic position within Asean. “These strengths provide a strong foundation for the development of a vibrant LAE ecosystem,” he said during his speech at the inaugural LAE Forum 2026 organised by the Civil Aviation Authority of Malaysia (CAAM) here.

items.title: Lim Guan Eng, wife to stand trial over RM11.6m Penang project-linked graft case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — DAP adviser Lim Guan Eng, his wife Betty Chew and businesswoman Phang Li Koon will stand trial on corruption and money laundering charges after the Court of Appeal dismissed their application to strike out the charges. The Court of Appeal’s decision clears the way for the trio to face trial over the charges. Lim is accused of using his position as then Penang chief minister and chairman of the Penang Development Corporation tender board to obtain gratification linked to the approval of a foreign workers’ hostel project worth RM11.6 million.

items.title: Some things you should know about Malaysia’s proposed new traffic law, including minimum RM500 speeding fine
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — The Malaysian government is proposing new changes to the law that will increase the minimum fine from RM300 to RM500 for driving without a driving licence and speeding, and also to introduce the new specific crime of road racing. The penalty proposed for first-time racing convictions will be a maximum RM10,000 fine or maximum two years’ jail or both; while convictions for the second or more offences will result in a maximum RM20,000 fine or maximum five years’ jail or both. Increasing minimum fine to RM500 for specific offences To improve compliance, the government is proposing a higher minimum fine of RM500 for offences such as: driving without a driving licence, or employing or allowing someone who has no driving licence to drive; or driving beyond the speed limit (speeding).
